title: Sucafina Market Report — 8 April 2026
content: This past week, coffee futures basis the most active May contract fell 12.35 cents from Tuesday to Tuesday to end at 286 US cents per pound. Most of this price action took place today at the time of writing. I expected last week for the market to reestablish a range of $2.90 to $3.00/lb for the week and this is what broadly occurred as geopolitically based speculative inflows balanced against increased selling interest from origin. Today's price action is a bit dramatic and began with a general risk-off environment following the US President's escalatory and inflammatory notes regarding the ongoing conflict with Iran. A more prolonged war can result in a broader commodity sell-off due to inflationary pressures caused by higher energy prices, ultimately impacting consumer spending. From the initial wave down, we broke below a critical 290 USc/lb support level, which indicates more selling from momentum funds nearby. As we look toward next week, most of the risk hinges on the Iran conflict, with further escalation being negative for prices. In other commodity news, Cocoa prices, which have been recently compared to coffee, have fallen to 136 US cents/lb, showing how brutal Bear cycles can become once supply concerns ease. My estimate for this week is for the market to try to test resistance at $2.75/lb.
